subject: Upgrade checkstyle to version 8.32 Google style
commit_message:
Upgrade checkstyle to version 8.32 Google style

This review upgrades the checckstyle in ONAP to version 8.32, and
re-aligns the ONAP style definition with the 8.32 version of the Google
style guide.

This change was triggered by incompatible changes in checkstyle version
8.32.

Checkstyle has removed the directives "allowMissingThrowsTag" and
"allowThrowsTagsForSubclasses". These directives controlled whether the existence
of @Throws tags should be checked or not. In ONAP, these directives were set
to "don't check".

The default behaviour in checkstyle now is not to check @Throws javadoc
tags for existence.

See: https://github.com/checkstyle/checkstyle/issues/7329

Issue-ID: INT-1271
Change-Id: Iaabbf05b6aaf244388f654aa4e965da3d3a4f4e9
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@est.tech>

entries.message:

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This change breaks the build, we need to match the Eclipse ONAP versions of checkstyle.

Patch Set 3:

By default, the oparent checkstyle configuration does not stop builds that fail checkstyle, so this change will not stop builds running.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Ok just to be sure, we are following Google Java code style? With the exceptions noted in this wiki:
https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Java+code+style

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> Ok just to be sure, we are following Google Java code style? With the exceptions noted in this wiki:
> https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Java+code+style

I didn't change anything in the java code style file except to remove the @Throws check directives which are not supported in the new checkstyle. So we're following the Google code style recommendations to the same extent as we were before.

I can check the ONAP code style file against the Google one to make sure.

Patch Set 3: -Code-Review

Here is the situation regarding the difference between the ONAP and Google Java style:

The ONAP Java style XML file is based on an old version of the Google style guide, the link quoted at the top of the ONAP Java style XML file file is below, but it no longer works:
https://google-styleguide.googlecode.com/svn-history/r130/trunk/javaguide.html (Dead link)

I did a diff of the ONAP style guide file against the latest Google one and they are substantially different. I'm guessing that the Google one has evolved over the last 3 or 4 years since ONAP branched it. See the differences below.

Examining the differences, the Google style guide has many additional checks.

Leaving aside these additional checks, the places where the ONAP one is different are:
- 120 characters rather than 100 per line
- Indentation of 4 ratehr than 2
- Don't check javadoc for files with names beginning with "test", "before" and "after"

So at this point, we have a different Checkstyle file when compared to the Google one.

Patch Set 3: -Code-Review

Ok - we need to get these checkstyle changes on both elalto and frankfurt branches also. elalto because our JDK8 projects haven't migrated yet to JDK11, this will help push them closer. And frankfurt just to keep that in synch.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: -Code-Review
> 
> Ok - we need to get these checkstyle changes on both elalto and frankfurt branches also. elalto because our JDK8 projects haven't migrated yet to JDK11, this will help push them closer. And frankfurt just to keep that in synch.

What should we do here:
1. Do the minimal to get past the removed directives
2. Bring in the new/stricter Google checks

I think #2, ONAP decided as a whole to match Google. Now is the time to upgrade as we are early in the release. Is that a major change??
